propositions: Epic and story acceptance criteria must state outcomes (children closed, work landed) rather than demanding child signatures.
Writing 'closed (signed)' in an AC restates the gate's own signature output as a criterion and defeats the certifiable:false soft-path escape hatch.
The signature-demand anti-pattern converted a PASS-but-unsigned soft path into a hard FAIL when a child was force-closed pending operator attestation.
Document the no-signature-demands-in-ACs rule in CLAUDE.md's ticket-template section and the relevant docs page with rationale.
Cross-reference bug 02a3 and the bf50/5f39 incident as the motivating case.
Optionally add a P-tier DET lint warning on 'signed' in a container ticket's AC checklist only if it fits an existing DET seam.
